strategic_profile.executive_summary: Cornell University is a prestigious Ivy League research institution distinguished by its unique hybrid structure, housing both privately endowed and state-supported statutory colleges. With over 100 majors across eight undergraduate colleges, it offers exceptional breadth, combining elite liberal arts and engineering programs with specialized, world-class schools in hospitality, agriculture, and industrial relations.
strategic_profile.market_position: Elite Private Ivy League / Public Ivy Hybrid

analyst_takeaways.insight: Cornell has ended its pandemic-era test-optional policy and will require the SAT or ACT for all applicants seeking enrollment for Fall 2026 and beyond.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants can no longer hide lower test scores; high standardized test scores (1510-1560 middle 50% SAT) will once again act as a hard filter for competitiveness.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Admissions are highly decentralized, with students applying directly to one of the eight undergraduate colleges rather than the university at large.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Major selection and college fit are crucial. Acceptance rates vary wildly between colleges (e.g., Dyson Business is <5%, while CALS may be closer to 15-20%).

analyst_takeaways.insight: Cornell maintains a strict need-blind policy for domestic students and meets 100% of demonstrated need with no-loan packages for families earning under $75,000.
analyst_takeaways.implication: While highly affordable for low-income domestic students, Cornell is need-aware for international students and does not offer any merit or athletic scholarships.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A large, diverse, and heavily pre-professional student body of over 16,000 undergraduates engaged in rigorous academics. Greek life is moderately prominent, with 24% of women and 27% of men participating.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Situated in rural Ithaca, the campus is relatively isolated but celebrated for its stunning natural gorges and vast footprint.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 Doctoral University with very high research activity, spending over $1.18 billion annually on R&D, heavily supported by the NSF and HHS.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional/test-free for Fall 2025 (varies by college). Mandatory SAT/ACT submission reinstated for all applicants for Fall 2026 and beyond.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admits heavily fluctuate based on yield.
Lowest waitlist admission year due to high yield during the pandemic.

gpa.notes: 85.6% of enrolled students were in the top 10% of their graduating class; 95.5% were in the top quarter.

testing.policy_note: Submission rate reflects the test-optional policy for the Class of 2028. Testing will be mandatory starting Fall 2026.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly quantitative students with perfect or near-perfect math scores and advanced STEM coursework.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Students must achieve a minimum grade of 'C' (not C-) in all CS and critical math courses, and average a 2.50 in CS 2110 and CS 2800 to affiliate.

core_courses: CS 2110 Object-Oriented Programming and Data Structures

majors.notes: Can also be pursued as a B.A. through the College of Arts and Sciences.

colleges.name: SC Johnson College of Business (Dyson School of Applied Economics and Management)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Future finance, consulting, and ag-business leaders; students wanting a highly selective, small cohort business program.

majors.name: Applied Economics and Management
notable_professors: Reza Moghimi (Data Skills and Database Management)
majors.notes: Administered jointly with the College of Agriculture and Life Sciences.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students interested in pure sciences, humanities, or double majoring across diverse disciplines.

majors.name: Biological Sciences

core_courses: BIOMG 1350 Introductory Biology: Cell and Developmental Biology

majors.notes: Also offered through the College of Agriculture and Life Sciences.

colleges.name: College of Human Ecology
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students interested in the intersection of human health, public policy, design, and social sciences.
colleges.student_archetype: Interdisciplinary thinkers focused on human-centric problems.

majors.name: Human Biology, Health, and Society
majors.special_requirements: Extremely competitive for internal transfer; requires a minimum 3.0 cumulative GPA with B or better in target courses.
majors.notes: One of the statutory (state-supported) colleges for NY residents.

colleges.name: College of Architecture, Art, and Planning
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly creative students with strong portfolios and a defined interest in design and urban spaces.

majors.name: Architecture
majors.notes: Internal transfer requires a 2.7 GPA minimum.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires college-specific supplemental essays that assess fit for the distinct undergraduate college applied to.
Required for external/internal transfers and first-year applicants to specific design majors.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Cornell heavily evaluates candidates based on their specific 'fit' for the exact undergraduate college they apply to.

major_selection_tactics: Apply to the state-supported colleges (CALS, Human Ecology, ILR) if your academic profile matches, as they can sometimes have slightly higher acceptance rates than Arts & Sciences or Engineering.
Avoid applying to the Dyson School or Computer Science if your math/quantitative profile is lacking, as these are heavily impacted and highly selective.
Match your high school transcript rigor strictly to your target college (e.g., advanced physics/math for Engineering, strong humanities for ILR).
college_ranking_tactics: Applicants must select a specific undergraduate college on their application, forcing them to commit to an academic pathway early.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Cornell allows applicants to indicate an alternate major or college choice, particularly for the College of Arts and Sciences and CALS, which can act as a fallback if the primary program is full.

financials.tuition_model: Differentiated tuition model based on state residency for statutory colleges, flat rate for endowed colleges.

scholarships.name: Cornell University Grant
scholarships.benefits: Institutional grant to meet 100% of demonstrated need; packages for families under $75k feature zero student loans.

ap_policy.general_rule: Requires a 5 on most AP exams for credit.
exceptions: Does not grant credit if the student's current institution granted credit for a 4 (specifically for transfer students).
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to fulfill prerequisites or skip introductory courses.

ib_policy.general_rule: High scores on Higher Level exams may yield credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: No more than 66 total transfer credits (including AP/IB) may be applied. Must have earned a C or better.

what_it_takes: Unweighted GPA near 3.9-4.0 with extreme course rigor.
Near-perfect standardized test scores, specifically in math for STEM majors.
A highly specific extracurricular profile that demonstrates fit for the targeted Cornell college.
common_activities: Sustained leadership in a few targeted clubs
essay_tips: Connect personal interests directly to Cornell's unique college-specific offerings.
Demonstrate intellectual vitality and problem-solving initiative.
Showcase 'any person, any study' ethos by crossing interdisciplinary boundaries.
red_flags: Applying to a specialized college without the specific high school prerequisites (e.g., Engineering without Physics/Calculus).
Generic essays that do not reference Cornell's specific structure or professors.
insights: Internal transfer is possible but highly competitive for impacted majors like Computer Science, requiring specific minimum GPAs in prerequisite 'weeder' courses.
